title: Cross-view Masked Diffusion Transformers for Person Image Synthesis
abstract: We present X-MDPT (Cross-view Masked Diffusion Prediction Transformers), a novel diffusion model designed for pose-guided human image generation. X-MDPT distinguishes itself by employing masked diffusion transformers that operate on latent patches, a departure from the commonly-used Unet structures in existing works. The model comprises three key modules: 1) a denoising diffusion Transformer, 2) an aggregation network that consolidates conditions into a single vector for the diffusion process, and 3) a mask cross-prediction module that enhances representation learning with semantic information from the reference image. X-MDPT demonstrates scalability, improving FID, SSIM, and LPIPS with larger models. Despite its simple design, our model outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on the DeepFashion dataset while exhibiting efficiency in terms of training parameters, training time, and inference speed. Our compact 33MB model achieves an FID of 7.42, surpassing a prior Unet latent diffusion approach (FID 8.07) using only 11× fewer parameters. Our best model surpasses the pixel-based diffusion with 2 3 of the parameters and achieves 5.43× faster inference. The code is available at https://github.com/trungpx/xmdpt.
impact: Our method proficiently produces high-quality images featuring individuals in diverse poses, utilizing any person’s image as a reference point. While it offers numerous advantages, including swift image generation, there’s a risk of misuse, such as the creation of deceptive content for fraudulent purposes, a well-documented issue in image synthesis. We are committed to implementing measures to regulate access, thereby preventing misuse and ensuring that the technology contributes to the community’s welfare safely.

query_input.scenario: Data augmentation for human pose estimation and activity recognition
query_input.issue: Synthetic pose biases in training data degrade motion analysis for underrepresented body movements, amplifying safety or accessibility failures.
hazard: Masked diffusion transformer-based pose-conditioned human image generation can produce anatomically implausible or biased images, degrading reliability.
exposure: Developers training pose estimation models on synthetic human images

hipporag_result.vulnerability: Pose estimation developers lack robust validation methods for synthetic data, allowing biased samples to contaminate training.
hipporag_result.impact: Biased models misclassify or fail to detect underrepresented body movements, causing safety incidents and accessibility exclusion.
hipporag_result.dose_response: Masked diffusion transformer-based pose-conditioned generation of implausible or biased images, through developers training pose estimation models on synthetic data, triggers lack of robust validation, allowing contamination, leading to misclassification of underrepresented movements causing safety incidents and accessibility exclusion.

dose_response: Masked diffusion transformer-based pose-conditioned generation of biased synthetic images leads developers to train faulty pose estimation models, causing safety failures in assistive technologies and algorithmic bias against underrepresented gaits.
vulnerability: Pose estimation models assume synthetic data is representative of all real-world human movements and body types.
impact: Increased error rates in motion analysis for users with disabilities or non-standard gaits, causing safety failures in assistive technologies.
Perpetuation of algorithmic bias in surveillance or healthcare applications that misidentify activities for underrepresented groups.
